title: Small Square Farmhouse Dining Table
summary: A compact square farmhouse table sized for one chair per side, with an interlocking X-stretcher for legroom.
description:
Most dining tables are built long first and legroom is an afterthought; this one is sized deliberately small, with legs spaced only 20" apart to fit one chair per side, which makes the X-stretcher's job harder — it has to brace a small footprint without the diagonal bracing getting in the way of anyone's knees.

The **X-stretcher pieces interlock with a half-lap notch cut into the center of each**, so the two pieces sit flush with each other instead of one crossing awkwardly over the top of the other. Cut this notch by hand with a saw and chisel, or with a jig-guided router if you'd rather not do it freehand.

The top attaches with **z-clips, not screws straight through the aprons.** Solid wood tabletops expand and contract across their width with humidity; z-clips let the top move slightly while still holding it down, where fixed screws would eventually crack the top or tear out.

tools.note: Or a circular or miter saw.
Cuts the X-stretcher notches by hand.

cutList.note: Optionally ripped to 3x3in for a slimmer look. Notch each leg for the X-stretcher starting 6in from the bottom, running up 2-1/2in.
Grooved on the inside face for z-clip attachment.
Half-lap notch in the center of each so they interlock flush.
Edge-glued into a roughly square top; adjust to your own square dimension.

steps.title: Cut and notch the legs
steps.body: Cut four legs from 4x4 stock, optionally ripping them down to 3x3in for a slimmer look. Cut an angled notch in each leg — starting 6in from the bottom, running up 2-1/2in — to accept the X-stretcher.

steps.title: Build the skirt assembly
steps.body: Cut the skirt boards to length, groove each for z-clip attachment, then pocket-screw and glue them between the legs.

steps.title: Build the interlocking X-stretcher
steps.body: Cut the X-stretcher pieces to length with a half-lap notch in the center of each so they interlock flush, glue them together, then pocket-screw the assembly to the legs.

steps.title: Build and attach the top
steps.body: Rip and edge-glue the tabletop boards into a roughly square panel, then attach it to the leg assembly using z-clips so the top can still expand and contract.
